currentFacts: Electron desktop pet platform with Control Center
PetService is the single source of truth for pet state
Control Center owns user-facing configuration
API keys stay in the main-process secret store

Third-party ecosystem docs frame packages as local extensions with legacy SDK compatibility, explicit setup execution for enabled policy-allowed local plugins, entries.commands support through the existing JavaScript compatibility runner plus explicit short-lived process execution for declaration-only local extensions, short-lived command bridge access for pet.say/pet.action/pet.event/read-only context, command result feedback in Control Center, explicit HTTP/HTTPS dashboard opening, explicit service start/stop controls without auto-start, manual loopback-only service health checks, opt-in host-managed periodic health checks for running services, command/setup/service spawning without shell expansion, best-effort process-group cleanup on service stop, exit-confirmed setup/command/service stop semantics, bounded host-side force stop for stubborn services, service-only descendant verification after requested stop, and honest non-sandbox language
Local HTTP and MCP are loopback-only and disabled by default
Codex pet directory and zip import are supported
Bundled pets include doro, duodong, and chispa

TypeScript migration baseline covers shared IPC, Control Center view contracts, the Control Center API facade, Control Center hook state boundaries, Control Center pane prop surfaces, main-process Control Center adapters for service/catalog/plugin/pet pack/About/update/actions payloads, plugin extension entry contracts, full release evidence archive / signed closure report contracts, and representative product payload fixtures

Packaged runtime smoke runner has archived real macOS packaged runtime evidence for automated launch, transparency, sprite, speech bubble, action playback, and built-in pack switching checks; desktop picker reports now include invalid-package feedback evidence, native picker evidence is still pending, Phase 65 requires packaged runtime readiness plus archive readiness to link the paired desktop picker smoke report, Phase 66 adds desktop picker evidence summary/archive manifest tooling for reviewed picker archives, and Phase 67 requires release-level archive and signed closure flows to consume that reviewed picker archive manifest

Release evidence archive manifest tooling and signed release closure reporting are available; the current archived closure report records official desktop, macOS, and Windows claims as not-ready until real signed evidence is complete
Current legacy plugin config is public settings; secret-like plugin config fields are rejected by the compatibility validator
Legacy SDK scaffolding is available through npm run create-openpet-plugin, including minimal, network, storage, and AI-assisted templates

Extension author docs are centered on plugin.json entries for setup, commands, services, dashboards, manifest declarations, optional config, and assets; current runtime explicitly runs setup entries for enabled policy-allowed local plugins, supports entries.commands through JavaScript compatibility packages with main, runs declaration-only local entries.commands as explicit short-lived processes with JSON stdin context, shows entry declarations and the latest command result summary in Control Center, injects short-lived bridge URL/token env vars for declaration-only commands, explicitly opens declared HTTP/HTTPS dashboards for enabled plugins, explicitly starts/stops declared service entries for enabled local plugins without auto-start, manually checks declared loopback service health endpoints, can host-manage periodic health checks for already running services through Control Center, spawns command/setup/service processes without shell expansion, attempts best-effort process-group cleanup on stop, only reports setup/command/service stop completion after child exit confirmation, adds one bounded host-side force-stop attempt for stubborn services, fails closed when host-visible service descendants survive a requested stop, and keeps universal process-tree cleanup guarantees as future work
Plugin sandbox evaluation recommends keeping the current child-process Node permission-model VM runner for v1.1 while documenting that it is not an absolute sandbox
Installed user pet packs can be exported as .openpet-pet.zip and re-imported with provenance metadata
AI behavior decisions are visible in Control Center with replay, redacted diagnostics export, and clear-history controls
Live documentation has been consolidated so README, HANDOFF, project-status-review, project-context, and v1.1 TODO status share the same current facts
The latest review-derived TODO design lives in docs/productization-next-steps-design.md
The Phase 38+ execution design lives in docs/productization-v1.1-todo-design.md

releaseNotes.humanFacing: Keep README and CHANGELOG short, factual, and free of phase-by-phase history.
releaseNotes.machineFacing: Use this file plus docs/project-documentation-design.md for quick programmatic context.
